hard_constraints.name: Operation Precedence
hard_constraints.description: A successor operation cannot start before all predecessors finish.
hard_constraints.validation: for each edge predecessor -> successor, predecessor.end_time <= successor.start_time

hard_constraints.name: Resource Capacity
hard_constraints.description: A unary equipment resource can process at most one operation at a time.
hard_constraints.validation: no overlapping intervals on the same unary resource

hard_constraints.name: Resource Capability
hard_constraints.description: An operation can only be assigned to a resource whose capabilities cover required capabilities.

hard_constraints.name: Calendar Availability
hard_constraints.description: Operations must be scheduled inside equipment/operator/material available windows.
hard_constraints.validation: operation interval is covered by all required calendars

hard_constraints.name: Material Availability
hard_constraints.description: Operations cannot start before required material is available.
hard_constraints.validation: operation.start_time >= material.available_at for all required materials

hard_constraints.name: Frozen Window
hard_constraints.description: Started, dispatched, or near-term operations inside the frozen window cannot be moved unless explicitly unlocked.
hard_constraints.validation: operation unchanged when operation.is_frozen is true

soft_constraints.description: Minimize tardiness for high-priority and critical customer orders.
Minimize unnecessary movement of unaffected operations.
Keep bottleneck resources fed while avoiding overload.
Reduce sequence-dependent setup or product family switches.
Prefer plans that are easier for shop-floor execution teams to understand and follow.
